parameters.content:
### On Telegram Event

Native push trigger — telegram calls this workflow the moment the event happens (no polling).

Next: Detect Sentiment

🔑 Telegram bot token

### Detect Sentiment

Scores the text's sentiment; ports fire positive / neutral / negative.

Ports: ①→ Escalate Negative — Telegram · ②→ Log Neutral Feedback · ③→ Thank the Sender

### Anthropic Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Detect Sentiment** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Escalate Negative — Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Collect All Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Log Neutral Feedback

Appends a new row to 'Document Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Collect All Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Thank the Sender

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Collect All Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Collect All Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Summarize long emails with Google Sheets and Telegram

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Summarize long emails with Google Sheets and Telegram. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual contracts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
On Telegram Event → Detect Sentiment → Escalate Negative — Telegram → Collect All Outcomes → Align Field Names → Send Summary to Telegram

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating contracts in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; Google OAuth2 / service account; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

A three-way branch on sentiment, not a single IF, because 'neutral' deserves different handling than 'negative' — quietly logging positive feedback while paging a human only for genuine complaints keeps the alert channel's signal-to-noise ratio high for whoever is on the other end.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a logging step to the neutral branch too and compare its volume against the negative branch.
2. Test borderline phrases through the sentiment node to see where the positive/neutral line actually falls.
3. Add a 4th branch for a specific emotion if your sentiment provider supports finer-grained labels.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your contracts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
